title: Rows that remain raw or suspend presentation
summary: Cursor/Visual rows, comments, static assertions, clang-format-off regions, macro-recording sessions, unsupported syntax, and cold large-buffer first paint deliberately avoid character-changing sugar.
details: The shared reveal set is described by BHV-001 and applies to frontend prefix conceals and triple-slash markdown as well as core declaration modules.
Rows whose first nonblank token belongs to a comment are treated as prose and left out of the C++ character transforms.
Every row covered by a static_assert declaration remains verbatim and is merely grayed so compile-time checks can be inspected without shorthand.
clang-format off through clang-format on is treated as intentionally hand-laid-out data and remains raw.
While a macro records, character-shifting presentation is suspended so recorded motions use real source columns; folding may remain active.
Large cold buffers show raw source until the deferred parser warm-up completes.
When parsing or balancing is uncertain, the renderer prefers raw source over a plausible but misleading transform.

provenance.note: Shared raw-row sets and cold state are computed centrally.
Recording enter/leave suspends and repaints the frontend stack.

evidence.reference: tests/frontend_interaction_spec.lua, comment_color_spec.lua, frontend_spec.lua static_assert/clang-format golden cases
evidence.claim: Reveal transitions and representative raw exclusions are regression-tested.

questions: Which rows deliberately bypass the frontend?
Why does macro recording temporarily show raw code?
What does the renderer do when syntax is ambiguous?

last_verified.method: Shared skipper, recording, cold gate, raw-fallback branches, and related tests reviewed.
